problem: If $\sqrt2 \sin 10^\circ$ can be written as $\cos \theta - \sin\theta$ for some acute angle $\theta,$ what is $\theta?$ (Give your answer in degrees, not radians.)

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what is meant by "If $\sqrt2 \sin 10^\circ$ can be written as $\cos \theta - \sin\theta$ for some acute angle $\theta$"?
classified_questions.label: f
